title: Xenial Comforting Spanish Pinto Beans Stew
description: A comforting and herbal Spanish dish featuring pinto beans, served in a stone bowl, perfect for a cold evening. This sugar free friendly stew is made with love and care to provide a delicious and nutritious meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the soaked and drained pinto beans, chicken broth, and chopped parsley to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, or until the beans are tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Traditionally, this stew is served in a stone bowl, which helps to retain the heat and add to the overall comforting experience.
To serve, place the stone bowl over low heat to warm it, then add the stew and serve immediately.
